command_examples: engineering, binary analysis, and wireless security research.

documentation.readme: The mission of this site is to provide reviews of USB WiFi adapters, links to specific adapters that are known to perform well with Linux ( see The Plug and Play List ), technical support and educational information.
usage_sections: Example WiFi 4 hostapd.conf for mt76 and rtw88 chipset based adapters and cards
Example WiFi 6 hostapd.conf for mt7921 and mt7922 chipset based adapters and cards
Example WiFi 7 hostapd.conf for mt7925 chipset based adapters and cards

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with eskil/penetrator-wifi (score=12); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with answeryt/Wifi-Lure (score=12); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with RAILGUN-Protocol/wifi-cracker (score=12); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

name: USB-WiFi
summary: The mission of this site is to provide reviews of USB WiFi adapters, links to specific adapters that are known to perform well with Linux ( see The Plug and Play List ), technical support and educational information.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need wireless_ble_ext, use USB-WiFi because The mission of this site is to provide reviews of USB WiFi adapters, links to specific adapters that are known to perform well with Linux ( see The Plug and Pla.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `engineering, binary analysis, and wireless security research.` as documented upstream for USB-WiFi.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Email: lucid_duck@justthetip.ca` as documented upstream for USB-WiFi.
When chaining wireless_ble_ext tools, pair USB-WiFi with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `USB-WiFi --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
